title: gymnadenia nigra subsp. gabasiana
intro: Gymnadenia nigra subsp. gabasiana is a subspecies of orchid native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe, characterized by its dark-colored flowers and unique growth habit. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits associated with Gymnadenia nigra subsp. gabasiana include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are believed to provide relief from various health complaints.
